# Iteration Plan

## Purpose
Plan goals, work items, and delivery commitments for an iteration in Kanban, Lean, or Scrum.

## Audience
Product Owner, Delivery Team, Scrum Master, Stakeholders.

## Inputs
- Risk-adjusted backlog (`docs/templates/risk/risk-adjusted-backlog-template-agile.csv`).
- Release plan (`docs/templates/schedule/release-plan-agile.md`).
- Capacity and WIP limits.

## 1. Iteration Metadata
- **Iteration name / number:**
- **Start date:**
- **End date:**
- **Delivery cadence:** (weekly, bi-weekly, flow-based)
- **Team / squad:**

## 2. Iteration Goals
List measurable outcomes for this iteration.

## 3. Work Items & Deliverables
| Item ID | Description | Priority | Owner | Acceptance Criteria | Definition of Done | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

## 4. Capacity & WIP Limits
| Team Member / Role | Capacity | WIP Limit | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |

## 5. Risks & Dependencies
- **Dependencies:**
- **Risks:**
- **Mitigation plan:**

## 6. Quality Checklist
- Automated tests updated.
- Definition of Done validated for each item.
- Compliance checks completed where applicable.

## 7. Review & Retrospective Plan
- **Demo/review date:**
- **Retro date:**
- **Carry-over actions:**

## Completion Checklist
- [ ] Iteration goals and deliverables documented.
- [ ] Capacity and WIP limits confirmed.
- [ ] Risks, dependencies, and quality checks recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Iteration plan aligns with prioritized backlog and delivery cadence.
- Stakeholders agree on goals and commitments.
